name: Gerry Mulligan
abstract: Gerald Joseph Mulligan (April 6, 1927 – January 20, 1996), also known as Jeru, was an American jazz saxophonist, clarinetist, pianist, composer and arranger. Though primarily known as one of the leading jazz baritone saxophonists—playing the instrument with a light and airy tone in the era of cool jazz—Mulligan was also a significant arr…

about_quotes.speaker: Chico Hamilton
about_quotes.evidence: Gerry and I hooked up and we had the first rehearsal for the quartet with Chet Baker, Bob Whitlock and myself... we just happened to be four guys at the right place at the right time.
about_quotes.doc_title: Chico Hamilton — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Butch Miles
about_quotes.evidence: I think I got slaughtered on that thing when I was working with Gerry Mulligan and a quartet back in the 80s, and I did a number of gigs with him and the big band
about_quotes.doc_title: Butch Miles interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, October 9, 2020 [interview 2 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Art Farmer
about_quotes.evidence: It turned out that he hired me for the gig... I worked with Mulligan for a year at least. We played mostly concerts... We're still very friendly.
about_quotes.doc_title: Art Farmer — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jay Leonhart
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] The list of people you’ve played with since then is most impressive: Gerry Mulligan you played with... [INTERVIEWEE] Yup, yup.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jay Leonhart interviewed by Monk Rowe, San Diego, California, February 15, 1998 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Jay Leonhart
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] The list of people you’ve played with since then is most impressive: Gerry Mulligan you played with... [INTERVIEWEE] Yup, yup.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jay Leonhart interviewed by Monk Rowe, New Hartford, New York, September 27, 2013 [interview 2 of 2] [transcript only]

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Goodwin
about_quotes.evidence: I worked with Gerry for a couple of years you know after Gary’s group broke up you know I played a lot with Gerry.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bill Goodwin interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, May 28, 2025
